reaction_equation.equation: dh/dt = c * Q * p_A * (1 - h) - P_d * h

sticking_coefficient.definition: combines the effects of all sequential and parallel surface processes

collision_flux.physical_meaning: Molecular collision flux to the surface
collision_flux.definition: Product of collision rate at unit pressure (Q) and partial pressure of reactant A (p_A)

annotation.uncertainty_note: `Q` is defined as collision rate at unit pressure (S00175), so the actual collision flux is `Q * p_A`.

site_density.physical_meaning: Adsorption density of molecules in saturation
site_density.definition: saturation areal density of adsorbed molecules

maximum_coverage.physical_meaning: Maximum fraction of occupied adsorption sites

annotation.uncertainty_note: Implied by the (1-h) term in the Langmuir model.

adsorption_rate_constant.physical_meaning: Effective adsorption rate constant for surface coverage
adsorption_rate_constant.definition: Product of sticking coefficient (c) and collision rate at unit pressure (Q)

annotation.uncertainty_note: This is the combined rate constant for the `(1-h)` term in the `dh/dt` equation, with `p_A` as the gas phase variable. The adsorption equilibrium constant `K` (1/Pa) is also a key parameter, defined as `K = c * Q / P_d` (E00013).

sticking_probability.definition: combines the effects of all sequential and parallel surface processes

growth_per_cycle.physical_meaning: Film growth per cycle (gpc); Saturation growth in cycle (gpc_sat)
growth_per_cycle.definition: gpc_sat is the equilibrium thickness of layer growth at saturation reactant exposure

growth_relation.growth_rate_expression: gpc = gpc_sat * K * p_A / (1 + K * p_A)
growth_relation.surface_growth_coupling: Film growth is calculated by solving the diffusion equation with surface reactions, where the growth per cycle (gpc) is related to surface coverage and saturation density.

reaction_dependencies.pressure_dependence: dependent on precursor partial pressure p_A

reaction_regime.reaction_order: First-order with respect to available sites (1-h) and gas pressure (p_A)
reaction_regime.coverage_dependence: adsorption rate decreases as surface coverage (h) increases

boundary_coupling.transport_reaction_coupling: The gas adsorption rate to the walls (g) acts as a sink term in the diffusion equation.

source_reference.paper_title: Modeling growth kinetics of thin films made by atomic layer deposition in lateral high-aspect-ratio structures
source_reference.notes: The model uses a dynamic Langmuir adsorption model. The adsorption equilibrium constant K (1/Pa) is a key parameter, defined as K = c * Q / P_d (E00013).
